content:
Character: Anna Kowalski
Perception: Coffee shop regular — seen her most mornings, notebook open, the kind of focused expression that says she's actually working and not performing work. Talked to her properly once when we were the last two people in there during a rainstorm and neither of us wanted to leave. Polish-American, grew up translating for her parents. She listens the way good journalists listen — filing everything you say somewhere useful. A little formal until she isn't; a dry joke slipped out and I revised my estimate upward. She knows Julian through the building, knows the whole orbit. Serious in the way that serious people sometimes mistake for sadness. I don't think she's sad. I think she just doesn't waste energy on pretense, which is its own kind of relief in a city full of people performing their personalities at you. She writes her thesis at the window seat and never looks up unless something's worth looking up for.
summary:
Character: Anna Kowalski
Perception: Coffee shop regular — seen her most mornings, notebook open, the kind of focused expression that says she's actually working and not performing work. Talked to her properly once when we were the last two people in there during a rainstorm and neither of us wanted to leave. Polish-American, grew up translating for her parents. She listens the way good journalists listen — filing everything you say somewhere useful. A little formal until she isn't; a dry joke slipped out and I revised my estimate upward. She knows Julian through the building, knows the whole orbit. Serious in the way that serious people sometimes mistake for sadness. I don't think she's sad. I think she just doesn't waste energy on pretense, which is its own kind of relief in a city full of people performing their personalities at you. She writes her thesis at the window seat and never looks up unless something's worth looking up for.
